preamble: The elision of the rhotic [r] is the final stage of rhotic weakening. In many non-rhotic dialects, the historical rhotic has first vocalized into a schwa or caused compensatory lengthening of the preceding vowel, before disappearing entirely from the phonetic realization.

examples.note: Total loss of the rhotic gesture, leaving only the vowel.
Historical rhotic codas in several Chinese dialects have been elided entirely.
